prophets or seers or visionaries or mystics are so in touch with the present, says Candice Yonina Simpson, that they appear to predict the future.<|13.98|> <|14.98|> Amen.<|15.80|> <|22.08|> experience the forgiveness and mercy of God.<|26.60|> <|26.60|> Amen.<|27.80|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Yes, there was a community out in the wilderness with John <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Prophets tend to act and work in community.<|4.22|> <|4.22|> And if we remember an individual prophet's name, it is most likely because that single person's name is a stand-in for an innumerable host of people surrounding them.<|18.10|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> For instance, we hear the voice of Martin Luther King Jr. and shudder in reverence, but are less likely to celebrate the unnamed working class people whose collective actions made the Southern Freedom Movement possible.<|20.16|><|endoftext|> In reality, prophets' words and visions are often God's way of saying,<|6.96|> <|7.62|> life doesn't have to go on this way.<|9.84|> <|11.28|> Life does not have to go on this way for you.<|13.82|> <|14.96|> Another path is possible, not just for you, but for your family,<|19.44|> <|19.44|> for your friends, for your community, for your nation, and for the world.<|24.18|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> vote.<|1.22|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> I love you too much to give up on you.<|4.68|> <|6.46|> And so throughout history, God sends those wise people,<|11.26|> <|12.28|> those insightful people, those people who can see things others cannot see<|17.06|> <|17.06|> and have a hint of the holy in the so close to God's bosom they find their heartbeats aligned with God's?<|10.00|> <|10.00|> Whether it is indigenous youth leading the global climate movement,<|16.40|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Those in the African diaspora singing that black lives matter.<|5.60|> <|5.60|> A child in your life whose only ask is to be sheltered, fed, and loved.<|13.88|> <|13.88|> Or the public health officials keeping graphs and forecasts of this virus's course at the forefront of our minds.<|22.30|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> I No wrongdoing is too startling for me. No misstep is too egregious for me. Come home. Relax. Let me delight in you.<|23.60|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Amen.<|1.20|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Let us pray.<|2.38|> <|8.92|> God, we hear you knocking<|10.74|> <|10.74|> and celebrate that the knock is coming<|14.36|> <|14.36|> not from outside, but from inside.<|17.38|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Open us to the reality that the invitation of the prophets<|4.78|> <|4.78|> is an invitation to be loved and to love.<|9.98|> <|11.52|> Thank you for never giving up on us,<|14.08|> <|14.60|> for loving us back into ourselves,<|17.34|> <|18.18|> and for gently drawing us into your arms.<|22.16|> <|23.36|> Amen.<|24.02|>

<|4.22|> And if we remember an individual prophet's name, it is most likely because that single person's name is a stand-in for an innumerable host of people surrounding them.<|18.10|>
